family_audit: Close grouped_rows=8 as a fresh negative family: it regressed to 24.517119 ms while preserving the same 200-reg / 2-CTA / 16.6%-warps machine state.
Keep the exact PTX winner as a recovery anchor, but do not rank another exact restore first because 24.164-24.183 ms is now a noise-band plateau rather than fresh headroom.
Deprioritize the non-PTX sibling for this round because it is a valid alternate surface but reproduces the same 48%-tensor / 16.6%-warps plateau.
Keep the old half-panel snapshots closed as-is, but reopen their low-register idea as a new, correctness-clean 256x128 family instead of replaying another broken historical commit.

directions.name: Force 3-CTA Residency On The PTX 128x128 Hot Band
directions.hypothesis: The latest grouped_rows=8 regression makes the core plateau constraint explicit. The hot-band PTX kernel is still the dominant kernel at about 32.69 us, but it runs with 200 registers per thread, 22,016 B shared memory per block, only 2 resident CTAs per SM, and therefore only 8 active warps per SM or about 16.6% achieved warps. That is a structural latency-hiding wall, not a DRAM wall. The next move should therefore restore the exact 489574e PTX winner surface and then aggressively tighten the hot-band PTX branch around __launch_bounds__ and obvious live ranges so ptxas is forced to target 3-CTA residency. If that succeeds, the hot-band kernel can move from 8 toward 12 warps per SM without reopening the already-settled grouped_rows or export-order branches.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Register-limited occupancy and latency hiding in the 128x128 PTX hot-band kernel, not global bandwidth.
directions.risk: Moderate. Correctness risk is low because the output ownership model stays on the proven PTX winner surface, but performance risk is real because forcing a tighter launch budget can introduce spills or expose a different barrier mix.
metrics_to_recheck: end-to-end median runtime with a >0.15 ms improvement threshold over the current 24.517119 ms run
ranking_notes: This is the highest-upside bounded change still compatible with one round of node_c work.
It directly attacks the measured 200-reg / 2-CTA residency wall instead of chasing another sub-0.1 ms PTX ordering difference.
The user guidance now explicitly treats <0.1 ms as noise, so the ranking favors structural occupancy moves over exact restore loops.
directions.stop_condition: Stop this family quickly if launch__registers_per_thread stays above about 190 or launch__occupancy_limit_registers remains 2 with no material runtime gain; improvements smaller than 0.10 ms should be treated as flat noise, not a real win.

directions.name: Transplant The Half-Panel Register Budget Into The Correct 256x128 Pivot
directions.hypothesis: The broken half-panel branch is not a candidate to replay as-is, but it did prove one important structural fact: the repo can reach about 92-93 registers per thread, about 32.9% active warps, and very low long-scoreboard stalls on a 256x128 hot-band geometry. The correct 256x128 pivot branch, by contrast, stayed near 167 registers, 16.7% warps, and 30 ms. The aggressive but still coherent next family is to start from the correctness-safe 256x128 pivot ownership path and selectively transplant the low-register staging and reuse ideas that made the half-panel branch interesting, while refusing to reintroduce the half-panel writer split that broke correctness.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Register footprint and steady-state staging efficiency inside the 256x128 hot-band path, plus barrier overhead created by the old half-panel ownership split.
directions.risk: High. This is the highest-ceiling family left in the repo, but it touches the same 256x128 control/export surface that already produced two correctness failures when ownership got too clever.
metrics_to_recheck: correctness on all 3 dataset cases before trusting runtime
ranking_notes: This family is kept above the exact restore because it is one of the only branches that ever moved the machine state off the 16.6%-warps plateau.
It is ranked second rather than first because the code motion needed to keep the low-register budget without reviving ownership bugs is substantially larger than the round-29 launch-bounds probe.
directions.stop_condition: Stop immediately if correctness fails again or if barrier stall stays above about 15% even after recovering low-register behavior; a fast-but-wrong 256x128 branch is not useful search progress.

directions.name: Restore The Best Measured PTX Grouping Window On The Accepted Surface
directions.hypothesis: The exact PTX winner remains the best correctness-proven anchor in the repo and should still be preserved as a recovery direction after the grouped_rows=8 regression. However, remeasuring it again is no longer the best primary use of a full round because the exact PTX restore and the non-PTX sibling now both sit in the same 24.16-24.18 ms noise band with the same 48%-tensor / 16.6%-warps machine state.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Known register-limited plateau on the accepted PTX hot-band surface.
directions.risk: Low. This is a recovery anchor, not a discovery move.
metrics_to_recheck: end-to-end median runtime versus the current grouped_rows=8 regression
ranking_notes: Keep this direction in the diagnosis so the frontier retains a safe recovery anchor.
Do not interpret another <0.1 ms difference inside the 24.16-24.18 ms band as meaningful progress toward the sub-20 ms target.
directions.stop_condition: Treat any result that lands back inside the current 24.16-24.18 ms plateau as a recovery-only step; do not spend the next round on another same-surface replay unless a new structural modification is attached.

notes: Round 29 switches the ranking rule from local plateau chasing to structure-first search. On the latest measured run, the hot-band PTX kernel still dominates at about 32.69 us with 200 registers per thread, 22,016 B shared memory per block, occupancy limit registers=2, and only 16.59% active warps. DRAM is only 9.77%, so the bottleneck is not bandwidth. The search therefore treats the 24.16-24.18 ms cluster as a noise-band plateau and ranks directions by whether they can break the residency wall instead of whether they can replay another small control-path delta.
